title: User, Agent, and Tool Authority, Least Agency, Dry Runs, and Revocation
description: The model inherits a service account, treats user intent as permission, uses write tools for read tasks, and cannot distinguish allowed, approval-required, or denied actions. Learners cumulatively produce least-agency authorization decision engine through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish authenticated user, affected subject, agent, application, tool, service identity, resource owner, and approver.
Issue task-scoped, resource-scoped, time-bounded capabilities and expose only tools permitted for the current state.
Classify pure reads, sensitive reads, reversible writes, irreversible writes, external communication, financial, identity, and security effects.
